titleStmt.title: The Acting Chairman of the American Delegation ( (Gibson) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Acting Chairman of the American Delegation ( Gibson ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
535. Since the receipt of your telegram No. 292, February 11, noon, Wilson and I have taken every possible measure to avoid raising the issue of any extension of the projected no-force affirmation to non-European states. These interventions were apparently successful because at the first meeting of the Political CommissionThe proceedings of this Commission are printed in Records of the Conference, Series D, vol. v, Minutes of the Political Commission. this morning
many speakers recognized the importance of confining the discussions on the no-force agreement and mutual assistance to European countries in the first instance. In particular this was recognized by Boncour, Bourquin,Member of the Belgian delegation to the General Commission. Madariaga,Member of the Spanish delegation to the General Commission; Ambassador to France. NadolnyHead of the German delegation to the General Commission. and Eden.Member of the British delegation to the General Commission; Under Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs.

After an able exposition by Boncour of the most practical method of approaching security from the simple to the complex, the Commission decided to begin its discussions of measures for increasing security in Europe by consideration of a limited no-force affirmation. The British delegate will submit a draftSee infra. covering this point before the meeting tomorrow.
